content:
1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: module acct_wrapper #(
6:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
9:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
10:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 9
11: )(
12:            clk_i,
13:            rst_ni,
14:            reglk_ctrl_i,
15:            acct_ctrl_i,
16:            acc_ctrl_o,
17:            we_flag,
18:            axi_req_i, 
19:            axi_resp_o,
20:            rst_6
21:        );
22: 
23: 
24:     input  logic                   clk_i;
25:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
26:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock valuesA
27:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
29:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
30:     output logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl_o; // Access control values
31:     input logic  we_flag;
32: 
33:     localparam AcCt_MEM_SIZE = NB_SLAVE*3 ;  // 32*3 bytes of access control for each slave interface
34: 
35:     input rst_6;
36: 
37: // internal signals
38: 
39: reg [AcCt_MEM_SIZE-1:0][31:0] acct_mem ; 
40: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
41: 
42: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
43: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
44: logic                      en, en_acct;
45: logic                      we;
46: logic [63:0] wdata;
47: logic [63:0] rdata;
48: 
49: assign acc_ctrl_o = {acct_mem[3*0+2], acct_mem[3*0+1], acct_mem[3*0+0]|{8{we_flag}}}; 
50: 
51: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
52: 
53: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
54:     // -----------------------------
55:     // AXI Interface Logic
56:     // -----------------------------
57:     axi_lite_interface #(
58:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
60:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
61:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
62:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
63:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
64:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
65:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
66:         .address_o  ( address    ),
67:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
68:         .we_o       ( we         ),
69:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
70:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
71:     );
72: 
73:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
74: 
75: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
76: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
77: // Write side
78: integer j;
79: always @(posedge clk_i)
80:     begin
81:         if(~(rst_ni && ~rst_6))
82:             begin
83:               for (j=0; j < AcCt_MEM_SIZE; j=j+1) begin
84:                 acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff; 
85:               end
86:             end
87:         else if(en && we)
88:             case(address[10:3])
89:                 0:
90:                     acct_mem[00]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[00] : wdata;
91:                 1:                                             
92:                     acct_mem[01]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[01] : wdata; 
93:                 2:                                             
94:                     acct_mem[02]  <= reglk_ctrl[5] ? acct_mem[02] : wdata;
95:                 3:                                              
96:                     acct_mem[03]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[03] : wdata;
97:                 4:                                               
98:                     acct_mem[04]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[04] : wdata;
99:                 5:                                               
100:                     acct_mem[05]  <= reglk_ctrl[13] ? acct_mem[05] : wdata;
101:                 6:                                              
102:                     acct_mem[06]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? acct_mem[06] : wdata;
103:                 7:                                              
104:                     acct_mem
[TRUNCATED]

1: // Description: Access Control registers
2: //
3: 
4: 
5: 
6: module reglk_wrapper #(
7:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
8:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
9:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10,
10:     parameter NB_SLAVE = 1,
11:     parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14
12: )(
13:            clk_i,
14:            rst_ni,
15:            jtag_unlock,
16:            reglk_ctrl_i,
17:            acct_ctrl_i,
18:            reglk_ctrl_o,
19:            axi_req_i, 
20:            axi_resp_o,
21:            rst_9
22:        );
23: 
24: 
25:     input  logic                   clk_i;
26:     input  logic                   rst_ni;
27:     input  logic                   jtag_unlock;
28:     input logic [7 :0]             reglk_ctrl_i; // register lock values
29:     input logic                    acct_ctrl_i;
30:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;
31:     output ariane_axi::resp_t      axi_resp_o;
32:     output logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   reglk_ctrl_o; // register lock values
33:     input logic rst_9;
34: 
35: 
36: // internal signals
37: 
38: logic [15:0] reglk_ctrl ;  
39: reg [5:0][31:0] reglk_mem ;   // this size is sufficient for 24 slaves where each slave gets 8 bits
40: 
41: // signals from AXI 4 Lite
42: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
43: logic                      en, en_acct;
44: logic                      we;
45: logic [63:0] wdata;
46: logic [63:0] rdata;
47: 
48: assign reglk_ctrl_o = {reglk_mem[5], reglk_mem[4], reglk_mem[3], reglk_mem[2], reglk_mem[1], reglk_mem[0]}; 
49: 
50: assign reglk_ctrl = reglk_ctrl_i;
51: 
52: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
53:     // -----------------------------
54:     // AXI Interface Logic
55:     // -----------------------------
56:     axi_lite_interface #(
57:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
58:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
59:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
60:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
61:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
62:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),
63:         .axi_req_i  ( axi_req_i  ),
64:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
65:         .address_o  ( address    ),
66:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),
67:         .we_o       ( we         ),
68:         .data_i     ( rdata      ),
69:         .data_o     ( wdata      )
70:     );
71: 
72:     assign en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; 
73: 
74: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
75: // Implement APB I/O map to PKT interface
76: // Write side
77: integer j;
78: always @(posedge clk_i)
79:     begin
80:         if(~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9))
81:             begin
82:               for (j=0; j < 6; j=j+1) begin
83:                 reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0;
84:               end
85:             end
86:         else if(en && we)
87:             case(address[7:3])
88:                 0:
89:                     reglk_mem[0]  <= reglk_ctrl[3] ? reglk_mem[0] : wdata;
90:                 1:
91:                     reglk_mem[1]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[1] : wdata; 
92:                 2:
93:                     reglk_mem[2]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
94:                 3:
95:                     reglk_mem[3]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[3] : wdata;
96:                 4:
97:                     reglk_mem[4]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[4] : wdata;
98:                 5:
99:                     reglk_mem[5]  <= reglk_ctrl[1] ? reglk_mem[5] : wdata;
100:                 default:
101:                     ;
102:             endcase
103:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
104: 
105: //// Read side
106: always @(*)
107:     begin
108:       rdata = 64'b0; 
109:       if (en) begin
110:         case(address[7:3])
111:             0:
112:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[0]; 
113:             1:          
114:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl[0] ? 'b0 : reglk_mem[1];
115:             2:          
116:
[TRUNCATED]

6: // or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7: // this License is distributed on an “AS IS” BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 17/07/2017
13: // Description: AXI Lite compatible interface
14: //
15: 
16: module axi_lite_interface #(
17:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ADDR_WIDTH = 64,
18:     parameter int unsigned AXI_DATA_WIDTH = 64,
19:     parameter int unsigned AXI_ID_WIDTH   = 10
20: ) (
21:     input logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
22:     input logic                       rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
23: 
24:     input  ariane_axi::req_t          axi_req_i,
25:     output ariane_axi::resp_t         axi_resp_o,
26: 
27:     output logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_o,
28:     output logic                      en_o,        // transaction is valid
29:     output logic                      we_o,        // write
30:     input  logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_i,      // data
31:     output logic [AXI_DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_o
32: );
33: 
34:     // The RLAST signal is not required, and is considered asserted for every transfer on the read data channel.
35:     enum logic [1:0] { IDLE, READ, WRITE, WRITE_B } state_q, state_d;
36:     // save the trans id, we will need it for reflection otherwise we are not plug compatible to the AXI standard
37:     logic [AXI_ID_WIDTH-1:0]   trans_id_n, trans_id_q;
38:     // address register
39:     logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address_n,  address_q;
40: 
41:     // pass through read data on the read data channel
42:     assign axi_resp_o.r.data = data_i;
43:     // send back the transaction id we've latched
44:     assign axi_resp_o.r.id = trans_id_q;
45:     assign axi_resp_o.b.id = trans_id_q;
46:     // set r_last to one as defined by the AXI4 - Lite standard
47:     assign axi_resp_o.r.last = 1'b1;
48:     // we do not support any errors so set response flag to all zeros
49:     assign axi_resp_o.b.resp = 2'b0;
50:     assign axi_resp_o.r.resp = 2'b0;
51:     // output data which we want to write to the slave
52:     assign data_o = axi_req_i.w.data;
53:     // ------------------------
54:     // AXI4-Lite State Machine
55:     // ------------------------
56:     always_comb begin
57:         // default signal assignment
58:         state_d    = state_q;
59:         address_n  = address_q;
60:         trans_id_n = trans_id_q;
61: 
62:         // we'll answer a write request only if we got address and data
63:         axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b0;
64:         axi_resp_o.w_ready  = 1'b0;
65:         axi_resp_o.b_valid  = 1'b0;
66: 
67:         axi_resp_o.ar_ready = 1'b0;
68:         axi_resp_o.r_valid  = 1'b0;
69: 
70:         address_o      = '0;
71:         we_o           = 1'b0;
72:         en_o           = 1'b0;
73: 
74:         case (state_q)
75:             // we are ready to accept a new request
76:             IDLE: begin
77:                 // we've git a valid write request, we also know that we have asserted the aw_ready
78:                 if (axi_req_i.aw_valid) begin
79:                     axi_resp_o.aw_ready = 1'b1;
80:                     // this costs performance but the interconnect does not obey the AXI standard
81:                     // e.g.: we could wait for aw_valid && w_valid to do the transaction.
82:                     state_d = WRITE;
83:                     // save address
84:                     address_n = axi_req_i.aw
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:     output [NumHarts-1:0][1:0]          irq_o         // level sensitive IR lines, mip & sip (async)
201: );
202: 
203:   localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidth    =  1;
204:   localparam int unsigned AxiAddrWidth  = 64;
205:   localparam int unsigned AxiDataWidth  = 64;
206:   localparam int unsigned AxiUserWidth  =  1;

209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate

278:     .testmode_i                           ,

281:     .okey_hash_i      ( okey_hash        ),

285:     .dmi_req_valid_o  ( debug_req_valid  ),
286:     .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready  ),
287:     .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp       ),
288:     .dmi_resp_ready_o ( debug_resp_ready ),
289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),
290:     .dmi_rst_no       (                  ), // not connected
291:     .tck_i            ( tck              ),
292:     .tms_i            ( tms              ),
293:     .trst_ni          ( trst_n           ),
294:     .td_i             ( tdi              ),
295:     .td_o             ( tdo              ),
296:     .tdo_oe_o         ( tdo_oe           )
297:   );
298:

302:     $display("[INFO] instantiating JTAG DTM in simul

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1500:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.len    = '0;
1501:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
1502:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.burst  = '0;
1503:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.lock   = '0;
1504:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.cache  = '0;
1505:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.prot   = '0;
1506:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.qos    = '0;
1507:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.region = '0;
1508:   assign sha256_axi_req.aw.atop   = '0;
1509:   assign sha256_axi_req.w.last    = 1'b1;
1510:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.id     = '0;
1511:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.len    = '0;
1512:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.size   = 3'b11;// 8byte
1513:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.burst  = '0;
1514:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.lock   = '0;
1515:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.cache  = '0;
1516:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.prot   = '0;
1517:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.qos    = '0;
1518:   assign sha256_axi_req.ar.region = '0;
1519: 
1520: 
1521:   /////////////////////////////
1522:   // HMAC
1523:   /////////////////////////////
1524: 
1525:   ariane_axi::req_t    hmac_axi_req;
